text: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear EH model and its corresponding resource allocation algorithm for SWIPT systems. Unlike the conventional linear EH model, the non-linear EH model is based on the practical non-linear EH circuit and considers its non-linear saturation region. With the proposed non-linear EH model, the resource allocation problem is formulated into a non-convex optimization problem, which maximizes the energy received by the energy receivers (ERs) and ensures the sufficient signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of the information receivers (IRs). The sum-of-ratios function is transformed into an equivalent subtractive form via convex modification techniques, which enables the design of an efficient iterative optimization algorithm. In each iteration, an optimal solution for the rank-constrained semidefinite program (SDP) can be obtained by solving a corresponding feasible SDP. Simulation results show that the proposed non-linear resource allocation has a better performance compared with linear resource allocation, which can alleviate the mismatch of resources in practice.
